See more detail about the changes, view the changes and submit your feedback on the : NIST invites comments on the initial public draft of SP 800-18r2, Developing Security, Privacy, and Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management Plans for Systems.

The public is invited to provide input by The NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF) provides a comprehensive, flexible, repeatable, and measurable 7-step process that any organization can use to manage information security and privacy risk for organizations and systems and links to a suite of NIST standards and guidelines to support implementation of risk management programs to meet the requirements of the Federal Information Security Modernization Act (FISMA).

, explains each RMF step, and offers resources to support implementation, such as updated Quick Start Guides, and the the organization to manage security and privacy risks the system and information processed, stored, and transmitted based on an impact analysis the set of NIST SP 800-53 controls to protect the system based on risk assessment(s) the controls and document how controls are deployed to determine if the controls are in place, operating as intended, and producing the desired results Senior official makes a risk-based decision to control implementation and risks to the system About the Risk Management Framework (RMF) Subscribe to the RMF Email Announcement List Federal Information Security Modernization Act Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management Open Security Controls Assessment Language Systems Security Engineering (SSE) Project.
